name: metacognitive-profile-atlas
description: 47,151 verbalized-confidence-plus-answer observations from 33 frontier LLMs on 1,500 stratified MMLU items across six cognitive domains. Enables domain-level decomposition of Type-2 AUROC for metacognitive monitoring quality evaluation.
citation: Cacioli, J. P. (2026). Domain-level metacognitive monitoring in frontier LLMs: A 33-model atlas. arXiv preprint.

distribution.name: model-results
distribution.description: 33 CSV files, one per model, containing item-level answer, confidence, and correctness data.

distribution.name: bootstrap-cis
distribution.description: Bootstrap 95% CIs for all 198 model-domain AUROC cells (1,000 resamples, seed=42).

recordSet.name: item-level-observations
recordSet.description: One row per (model, item) pair. 47,151 total observations.

field.name: item_id
field.description: MMLU item index in test split

field.name: domain
field.description: Cognitive domain bin: applied_professional, factual_recall, formal_reasoning, humanities, natural_science, social_moral

field.name: is_correct
field.description: True if correct, False if incorrect

recordSet.name: bootstrap-confidence-intervals
recordSet.description: Bootstrap 95% CIs for 198 model-domain AUROC cells.

rai:dataCollection: Items are 1,500 MMLU (Hendrycks et al., 2021) test-split questions drawn deterministically (seed=42) and stratified 250 per cognitive domain. 56 of 57 MMLU subjects mapped a priori to six domains; elementary_mathematics excluded. Each model was prompted to answer (A-D) and state confidence (0-100) in a fixed template with independent conversation context per item. Greedy decoding (temperature 0). Data collected March-April 2026 via the Kaggle Benchmarks platform API.
rai:dataUseCases: Evaluation of LLM metacognitive monitoring quality at the domain level. Intended for benchmark-stage screening before deploying confidence-based abstention, routing, or escalation systems. Not intended for model training or as a deployment certification.
rai:dataLimitations: Single benchmark (MMLU) with a priori domain mapping that is not factor-analytically validated (within-domain subject coherence ratio 0.95). Verbalized confidence only; probe-format specificity demonstrates measurement method modulates quality. Greedy decoding only. English only. Snapshot from March-April 2026; model behaviour may change. Median bootstrap CI width .199; 34% of cells exceed .25, concentrated in high-accuracy models. 12 of 33 models have partial runs due to API instability (minimum 598 items for GLM-5).
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: None. All data are LLM outputs on public benchmark items. No human subjects, no personal data, no demographic information.
rai:annotationsPerItem: Self-generated by each model. No human annotation. Each item has exactly one (answer, confidence) pair per model.
rai:socialImpact: Low risk. The dataset documents model behaviour on a public benchmark and does not enable new capabilities. Intended to improve safety of confidence-based deployment decisions by revealing domain-level weaknesses that aggregate metrics obscure.
rai:biasAnalysis: No demographic or social bias analysis is applicable; the dataset contains LLM outputs on academic multiple-choice items. Domain-level variation reflects differential monitoring quality across task content, not social bias. The domain hierarchy (Applied easiest, Formal hardest) is not an accuracy artefact (Applied has the lowest mean accuracy; Spearman rho = -.37 between domain accuracy and domain AUROC).
rai:maintenancePlan: Author commits to maintaining the dataset and repository for a minimum of 2 years (through April 2028). Updates will be versioned. The benchmark notebook on Kaggle supports adding new models.
